description: A retried operation runs its side effect twice, producing duplicate charges, emails, or rows.
body: Failure mode: a client or queue retries after a timeout, but the operation has no dedup, so the effect (charge, send, insert) executes again. Detection signals: no idempotency key or unique constraint on the write; the response that timed out actually succeeded server-side; duplicate rows/charges appear under retry. Do not rely on the network delivering exactly once. Safer fallback: add an idempotency key recorded in the same transaction as the effect, short-circuit on replay, and add a replay test asserting a single effect.
